vocab.meaning: phương thức giải tỏa sự sáng tạo
vocab.example: Painting served as a vital creative outlet for me during my formative years at school.

vocab.meaning: sự am hiểu về nghệ thuật
vocab.example: Our teachers aimed to build our artistic literacy by introducing us to different movements like Impressionism.

vocab.meaning: nắm vững những kiến thức cơ bản
vocab.example: In my early art classes, we had to master the fundamentals of perspective and shading before moving to color.
I vividly remember the tactile experience of finger painting, which was incredibly messy but exhilarating.
One of our assignments was to try and replicate a masterpiece to understand the artist's brushwork.

vocab.meaning: hình ảnh hóa các khái niệm
vocab.example: Learning to draw helped me learn how to visualize concepts, which eventually benefited my science studies too.
My parents noticed I had an artistic temperament because I would spend hours perfecting a single sketch.

vocab.meaning: những nét vẽ đầy biểu cảm
vocab.example: I was encouraged to use bold and expressive strokes rather than worrying about making everything look perfect.
Even as a child, I tried to use dark blues and purples to evoke a sense of mystery in my landscape paintings.
I always gravitated towards a vibrant palette because I felt it better reflected my energetic personality back then.
